data.title: As a developer, an integration test fixture verifies autonomous recovery from an interrupted-session state
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Creates an integration test fixture (in tests/) that stages a realistic interrupted-session scenario — a task ticket with an unmatched WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comment pointing to a real git branch with unmerged commits — and verifies that the resume logic produces an autonomous merge or discard decision without requiring manual git commands.

**Why**: The resume logic in Stories C-Foundation and C-Enhancement touches multiple code paths (git state inspection, ticket comment scanning, merge/discard decisions) that are difficult to validate with unit tests alone. An integration fixture provides end-to-end confidence that the full pipeline works correctly.

**Scope**:
- IN: Integration test fixture that initializes a real temp-dir git repository (not mocked git commands), writes a WORKTREE_TRACKING:start ticket comment, creates a branch with unmerged commits, invokes the resume scan logic, and asserts that the outcome is an autonomous merge or discard without any manual git commands; test must be deterministic (no random branch names or timestamps)
- OUT: Full sprint/fix-bug skill execution (too heavyweight for integration test); competing-implementation multi-branch path (covered by C-Enhancement unit tests)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, an integration test in tests/ stages an interrupted-session state (task ticket with unmatched WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comment pointing to a branch with unmerged commits) and asserts that the resume scan logic produces an autonomous merge or discard decision without any manual git commands
  ← Satisfies: "An integration test fixture stages an interrupted-session state (a task ticket with an unmatched WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comment pointing to a branch with unmerged commits) and verifies that resuming the ticket results in an autonomous merge or discard decision without any manual git commands."

- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] The integration fixture must use `git init` in a temp directory rather than mocking git commands so that the actual merge/discard logic is exercised against real git state
- [Reliability] The fixture must be deterministic — use fixed branch names, fixed commit messages, and a fixed timestamp seed; any randomness will cause flaky test failures
- [Testing] The fixture should cover both the merge path (branch has unique commits, no conflicts) and the discard path (branch is already merged) as two distinct test cases

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
